summary: Iteration 5: validated the server-side temp-caching / upload_id flow architecture change. Updated all 41 prior tests in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and /app/backend/tests/test_chunked_storage.py to use the new upload_id-based save/remove-duplicates contract (old cleaned_data payload would 422). Added 9 fresh tests in /app/backend/tests/test_upload_id_flow.py covering: upload response shape (no cleaned_data/original_data; contains upload_id/preview_data/total_rows etc.), upload response size <100KB even for 19,418x41 rows (measured 60,115 bytes via browser), save body size <2KB (measured 781 bytes), 404 on unknown upload_id with friendly 'Upload session not found or expired' message, 409 idempotency on second save with same upload_id, NaN sanitization (missing numerics returned as null, no NaN literal in JSON), TTL index on dataset_rows (created_at, 1) with expireAfterSeconds=3600 + partialFilterExpression status=draft, /rows excludes draft chunks (404 on unsaved upload_id), and the spec'd skip=15000&limit=3 pagination on 19,418-row dataset (total=19418, row[0]=R15000). Full pytest run: 50 passed, 3 skipped (all 3 skips disappear when MONGO_URL is exposed; verified passing with env loaded — TTL index check passes). Frontend Playwright (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL): full upload-to-dashboard happy path with the 2k-row CSV — Upload CSV modal → file set → DataQuality reached in 1.8s → Proceed to Dashboard fires POST /api/datasets/save (781-byte body) → 200 in 0.3s → dataset-selector-button shows new name → KPI cards (CSAT Score, Survey ID, Ticket ID, Reservation ID, Genius Level, CSAT, DSAT, Survey #) and metric chart cards (Ticket ID, Reservation ID, etc.) render. The user-reported P0 bug 'Proceed to Dashboard button still not working' is RESOLVED end-to-end.

issues: textContent flattening still reads 'DatasetTEST_csat2k_1781944494' (the small 'Dataset' caption is glued to the bold name without separator). Already flagged in iteration 4 — cosmetic, not blocking.

critical_code_review_comments: datasets_routes.py: clean two-phase design — _store_chunks(status='draft') + _load_all_draft_rows + atomic-ish promotion via update_many($unset). Reusing upload_id as dataset.id is clever (chunks already point at the final ID, no copy needed).

_sanitize correctly walks nested dicts/lists and substitutes None for NaN/Infinity. Verified via test_nan_in_numeric_column_returned_as_null and a content-level check that 'NaN' literal never appears in the JSON body.
db.py: TTL index spec is correct — (created_at, 1) + expireAfterSeconds=3600 + partialFilterExpression={status: 'draft'} ensures committed chunks are immune. Verified live against MongoDB.
Idempotency check uses db.datasets.find_one({id: upload_id}) before promotion → 409 'already saved'. Good.

Minor: remove_duplicates does delete_many + insert_many sequentially (not atomic). If a request is killed between delete and insert, the draft chunks are lost and the user has to re-upload. Acceptable trade-off given TTL safety net, but a transaction (or a status='draft_pending_replace') would be more robust.
Minor: SaveDatasetRequest does not validate that upload_id is a UUID format. Currently any non-existent string yields 404 (acceptable), but a Field pattern would make the API contract clearer.

Frontend Dashboard.handleSaveDataset: posts {upload_id, name, numeric_columns, label_columns, date_columns, quality_score} — verified 781 bytes for the 2k CSV save. loadSampleData → CSV blob → /upload pipeline unification is a nice consolidation.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (updated _create_dataset, sample_dataset_id, test_dataset_delete, test_remove_duplicates_*, test_datasets_sorted_newest_first to use upload_id flow; added test_remove_duplicates_unknown_upload_id)
/app/backend/tests/test_chunked_storage.py (updated large_dataset fixture + cascade + small_compat to upload_id flow)

success_rate.backend: 100% (50/50 passing; 3 pymongo-env-gated tests skip without MONGO_URL, all pass when env is exposed)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (P0 upload→proceed→dashboard verified end-to-end; round-trip sizes 60KB/781B within budget)

seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed datasets with autocleanup via session.delete. UI flow created TEST_csat2k_<ts> and left it for the user to inspect (can be deleted from UI).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 verifies the architectural pivot to server-side temp caching (upload_id flow). All 41 prior tests were migrated, 9 new tests added. The real /tmp/csat.csv (11MB / 19,418 rows) IS present in this env now — used /tmp/csat_2k.csv for the browser test (light memory) and a synthetic 19,418x41 inside pytest for the size-budget test. The /api/datasets/upload endpoint enforces MAX_ROWS=50_000 (413), CHUNK_SIZE=5000, PREVIEW_ROWS=50. Save endpoint reuses upload_id as dataset.id and flips draft→committed via $unset. TTL index expireAfterSeconds=3600 with partialFilter status=draft auto-reaps abandoned uploads. For future runs: if the user attaches a CSV with >50k rows, the upload returns 413 with a friendly summarization tip.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause of the recurring 'Proceed to Dashboard not working' P0 bug: the OLD save flow re-sent the full cleaned_data + original_data over the wire (~50MB JSON for the user's 19,418-row CSV), which exceeded browser memory + request body limits + fetch reliability thresholds. Fix verified: upload now caches the cleaned data server-side as 'draft' chunks (TTL 1h via Mongo index) and returns ONLY metadata + 50-row preview + upload_id (60KB instead of 50MB). Save references just the upload_id and promotes the draft chunks in place (no copy). Frontend now sends 781-byte save bodies regardless of dataset size. Verified end-to-end in a real browser session: upload→DataQuality→Proceed→dashboard switched with new dataset; save returned 200 in 0.3s.
